resources.string: On some devices while granting this permission android might show you a 10-second security countdown, this is for security purposes and you can safely grant them.
PrivaScope shows you what your apps are doing that includes screen time, storage, permissions and more. It is 100% private, local, and your data never leaves this phone.
We need the "Screen time" permission to show these to you.
We need the "Read notification" permission to show these to you.
We need the "Read storage" permission to show these to you."

Android will show a security screen with a 10-second countdown. This is completely normal — Android does this for all privacy apps.\n\nJust wait 10 seconds, find PrivaScope in the list and toggle it ON.
You can grant %1$s later from the Settings page inside PrivaScope.
Your daily and weekly screen time
How many times you open each app
Storage size of each installed app
Which apps notify you the most

PrivaScope helps you understand what\'s happening on your phone. See which apps use your camera, drain your storage, or sit unused with sensitive permissions. Everything runs on your device and nothing is ever shared.

PrivaScope builds your privacy report over time. The app cannot access many of the previous data and it starts collecting them the day you install it. Live usage stats may reset after a restart due to Android system limits, but your historical reports remain safe.
Needed to show screen time, app launches and weekly activity.
Allows PrivaScope to show how much space on your device is being used.
We use it to count notification sent by each app along with notification swiped/opened. No notification content is ever read.
It allows PrivaScope to see all apps on your device and is granted after the install.
Used to check what permissions each app has and is granted after the install.
Allows PrivaScope to read mobile and Wi-Fi data consumption by each app.
How we measure your screen time and app activity
How we find which apps have sensitive access
How we calculate how much space each app is using
How we count your daily phone unlocks and notifications
How we identify apps that may be a risk to your privacy
How PrivaScope keeps your data fresh without draining battery
Turning this off will stop screen time tracking and most app analysis features.
Turning this off will hide the Photos, Videos and Music storage breakdown.
Turning this off will stop tracking how many notifications apps send you.
How we measure your screen time
We track how long each app is open on your screen every day. This data gets updated the moment you open the app and shown in your weekly chart.
Every time you open an app, we count it. This helps you see which apps you reach for the most.
If an app hasn\'t been opened in 30, 60 or 90 days, we flag it. These apps often have sensitive permissions.
We find your longest continuous screen-on and screen-off periods to show your phone usage patterns throughout the day.
All your local data is encrypted using SQLCipher. This means even if someone gets access to your phone\'s internal files, they can\'t read your PrivaScope history.
We don\'t collect names, emails, or device IDs. To PrivaScope, you are completely anonymous and we have no way to link data to you.
How we find which apps have sensitive access
We read every permission each app has been given that includes Camera, Microphone, Location and Contacts and show you the full list.
Apps with special system access like reading notifications or permission to install other apps or accessibility permissions are highlighted separately as they have more power over your device.
Any app installed in the last 7 days is listed separately.
While the source code is on GitHub for transparency, you agree not to reverse engineer or modify the app for malicious use or to create harmful derivatives.
By using this app, you confirm you are at least 13 years old or have reached the minimum age of digital consent in your region.
The developer is not responsible for the privacy practices or content of third-party websites linked within the app, such as GitHub.
How we calculate how much space apps are using
We read your phone\'s total, used and free storage directly.
For each app we calculate three things: the app\'s install size, data accumulated by the app, and its temporary cache files.
Cache is temporary data apps save to load faster. It is safe to clear and does not delete any of your personal files or settings.
With optional storage permission, we also show how much space your media files are taking up and are broken down by type.
How we count your daily activity
Every time you unlock your screen, we count it. This gives you a simple picture of how often you pick up your phone.
We count how many notifications each app sends you using Android\'s usage system. We never read or store the content of any notification.
We show how much mobile and Wi-Fi data each app has used today. No actual network traffic is monitored or stored.
Unlock and notification counts reset every day. They show your activity for the current day only.
How we identify privacy risks on your phone
We show you exactly how many apps have access to your location, camera and microphone. Tapping any of these shows the full list of those apps.
Apps with access to your contacts, messages and call history are listed separately as these are among the most personal types of data.
We combine unused app data and permission data to highlight the apps that pose the biggest risk like an old app that still has your camera access.
We track which apps were installed, updated or removed this week so you always know what changed on your phone recently.
How PrivaScope keeps your data fresh
Once a day, PrivaScope quietly updates your app list, storage data and usage history in the background. You don\'t need to do anything.
We use Android\'s WorkManager which is designed to run tasks efficiently without draining your battery or slowing your phone.
When you open PrivaScope, it shows your last saved data immediately while quietly fetching any updates in the background.
PrivaScope does not need internet to work. All data is read directly from your device and stored locally.
All analysis including screen time, permissions, storage happens entirely on your device. Nothing is ever sent anywhere.
PrivaScope has no third-party SDKs. We have no way to track or store any data.
PrivaScope does not have internet permission. It is technically impossible for it to send any data anywhere.
Uninstalling PrivaScope removes everything. No data remains on your phone or anywhere else after uninstall.
PrivaScope is completely free. There are no subscriptions, no in-app purchases and no hidden charges.
PrivaScope shows you information about your device. It does not make any changes to your apps, settings or data on your behalf.
We do our best to keep data accurate but some readings may vary depending on your device model or Android version.
Any action you take based on PrivaScope\'s information like revoking a permission is your own decision. We are not responsible for any outcome.
PrivaScope is built using the following open source libraries. We are grateful to their developers.
All libraries above are licensed under Apache License 2.0, to view full license %1$s
These apps were not installed from an official store.
All systems look secure. No immediate action required.
No high-risk permissions detected. This app is safe.
Enable storage access to see media breakdown
No usage recorded for this day
These apps have one or more of the following permissions. To see exactly which ones are being used, open the app\'s detailed view.
Allows the app to access your precise location using GPS and network sources (WiFi/Cell towers).
Allows the app to access your approximate location using network sources only.
Allows the app to access your location even when you are not actively using the app.
Allows the app to read all SMS messages stored on your device.
Allows the app to receive and process incoming SMS messages.
Allows the app to send SMS messages from your phone without your manual intervention.
Allows the app to monitor or receive WAP push messages sent by your service provider.
Allows the app to receive and process incoming multimedia messages (MMS).
Allows the app to read data about your contacts, including phone numbers and email addresses.
Allows the app to modify your existing contacts or create new ones.
Allows the app to see the list of accounts (Google, WhatsApp, etc.) currently logged into your device.
Allows the app to access phone features, including your phone number and current cellular network info.
Allows the app to initiate a phone call automatically without going through the Dialer screen.
Allows the app to read your entire phone call history.
Allows the app to modify or delete entries in your phone\'s call history.
Allows the app to add messages to your system voicemail inbox.
Allows the app to make or receive internet-based (VoIP) calls.
Allows the app to see the number you are dialing and potentially redirect or abort the call.
Allows the app to take pictures and record videos using your device\'s camera hardware.
Allows the app to access the microphone to record audio or listen to voice input at any time.
No apps used more than 1MB today.

At PrivaScope, we categorize these as \'Highly Sensitive\' because they allow apps to go beyond normal permissions. While trusted tools use these for advanced features, they require extra caution because they have a \'bird’s-eye view\' of your phone\'s activity.
Acts as a \'Screen Helper\'. Essential for password managers to auto-fill or screen readers for assistance, but it can see everything you type.
Acts as a \'Message Reader\'. Needed for Smartwatches to show alerts on your wrist, but it can also read private OTPs from your notifications.
Creates \'Floating Windows\'. Used by apps like \'Where is my train\' or Messenger bubbles to show info over other apps. Be careful as it can show fake screens.
Gives \'Master Control\'. Useful for \'Find My Device\' to lock your phone if lost, but it\'s powerful enough to wipe your data or change locks.
Acts as an \'External Installer\'. Lets apps like Chrome download and install other apps. Dangerous if a random app installs something without asking.
Acts as a \'System Editor\'. Automation apps use this to change your brightness or DND mode automatically, but it can modify core system behaviors.
Acts as an \'Internet Guard\'. Secures your connection on public Wi-Fi, but it means the app can see and route all your internet traffic.
See which apps send the most alerts and audit sensitive message access. We never read, store or use any of your private data. You can always change from settings.
To audit which apps are reading your notifications (like potential spyware), PrivaScope needs Notification Access.

Important: You might see a 10-second safety timer on your device settings. This is standard for security focused tools. We never read your private content and we only use it to count alerts.\n You can always remove this from the settings.
It only takes minutes to set up.

Enable permission to unlock detailed data usage, app Notifications and total Screen unlocks. We only track timestamps and never read, store or use any of your private data. You can always change from settings.
To show your usage habits, PrivaScope needs \'Usage Access\'.

Important: You might see a 10-second safety timer on your device settings. This is for security purposes and you can safely grant them. We never read your private content.\n You can always remove this from the settings.

Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Nam in scelerisque sem. Mauris volutpat, dolor id interdum ullamcorper, risus dolor egestas lectus, sit amet mattis purus dui nec risus. Maecenas non sodales nisi, vel dictum dolor. Class aptent taciti sociosqu ad litora torquent per conubia nostra, per inceptos himenaeos. Suspendisse blandit eleifend diam, vel rutrum tellus vulputate quis. Aliquam eget libero aliquet, imperdiet nisl a, ornare ex. Sed rhoncus est ut libero porta lobortis. Fusce in dictum tellus.

 Suspendisse interdum ornare ante. Aliquam nec cursus lorem. Morbi id magna felis. Vivamus egestas, est a condimentum egestas, turpis nisl iaculis ipsum, in dictum tellus dolor sed neque. Morbi tellus erat, dapibus ut sem a, iaculis tincidunt dui. Interdum et malesuada fames ac ante ipsum primis in faucibus. Curabitur et eros porttitor, ultricies urna vitae, molestie nibh. Phasellus at commodo eros, non aliquet metus. Sed maximus nisl nec dolor bibendum, vel congue leo egestas.

 Sed interdum tortor nibh, in sagittis risus mollis quis. Curabitur mi odio, condimentum sit amet auctor at, mollis non turpis. Nullam pretium libero vestibulum, finibus orci vel, molestie quam. Fusce blandit tincidunt nulla, quis sollicitudin libero facilisis et. Integer interdum nunc ligula, et fermentum metus hendrerit id. Vestibulum lectus felis, dictum at lacinia sit amet, tristique id quam. Cras eu consequat dui. Suspendisse sodales nunc ligula, in lobortis sem porta sed. Integer id ultrices magna, in luctus elit. Sed a pellentesque est.

 Aenean nunc velit, lacinia sed dolor sed, ultrices viverra nulla. Etiam a venenatis nibh. Morbi laoreet, tortor sed facilisis varius, nibh orci rhoncus nulla, id elementum leo dui non lorem. Nam mollis ipsum quis auctor varius. Quisque elementum eu libero sed commodo. In eros nisl, imperdiet vel imperdiet et, scelerisque a mauris. Pellentesque varius ex nunc, quis imperdiet eros placerat ac. Duis finibus orci et est auctor tincidunt. Sed non viverra ipsum. Nunc quis augue egestas, cursus lorem at, molestie sem. Morbi a consectetur ipsum, a placerat diam. Etiam vulputate dignissim convallis. Integer faucibus mauris sit amet finibus convallis.

 Phasellus in aliquet mi. Pellentesque habitant morbi tristique senectus et netus et malesuada fames ac turpis egestas. In volutpat arcu ut felis sagittis, in finibus massa gravida. Pellentesque id tellus orci. Integer dictum, lorem sed efficitur ullamcorper, libero justo consectetur ipsum, in mollis nisl ex sed nisl. Donec maximus ullamcorper sodales. Praesent bibendum rhoncus tellus nec feugiat. In a ornare nulla. Donec rhoncus libero vel nunc consequat, quis tincidunt nisl eleifend. Cras bibendum enim a justo luctus vestibulum. Fusce dictum libero quis erat maximus, vitae volutpat diam dignissim.
